successCriteria.description: src/modules/status/status.routes.ts exists and exports a named async function statusRoutes(fastify: FastifyInstance): Promise<void> that registers GET /status
src/modules/status/index.ts re-exports SystemStatus, IStatusService, StatusService, and statusRoutes
tests/unit/modules/status/status.test.ts exists with a test verifying GET /status returns HTTP 200 with JSON body { up: true, version: '1' }
The route handler catches service errors and returns HTTP 500 with { up: false, version: '1' }
All imports reference only Phase 1 files (status.model.ts, status.service.interface.ts, status.service.ts) or files created in this phase — no circular dependencies

constraints: No sensitive data in logs: the /status route must not log passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data
Error handling: the route handler must catch all async errors and return a controlled 500 response — no unhandled promise rejections
All imports must resolve to files that exist in the project (Phase 1 files already on disk)
No @gestalt/* packages in any generated code
The route plugin must follow Fastify plugin conventions (async function receiving FastifyInstance)
outOfScope: Any database access, repository pattern, or persistence — this is a pure in-memory status check
RBAC enforcement on GET /status — the intent does not mention authentication or authorization for this health-check endpoint
Input validation — GET /status has no request body or query parameters to validate
Audit records — no state-changing operations occur
Phase 1 files (status.model.ts, status.service.interface.ts, status.service.ts) — these already exist and are not modified
Any other modules (leave, balance, employee, policy, notification, audit)

ambiguities.description: Test framework name mismatch: the intent text says 'Vitest' but HARNESS.json declares 'Jest' as the test framework, and the per-phase architecture code uses Jest APIs (jest.mock, jest.fn, jest.Mock). The per-phase architecture code is authoritative for implementation.
options: Use Jest as declared in HARNESS.json and shown in per-phase architecture code
Use Vitest as stated in the intent prose

ambiguities.description: Test file location mismatch: the intent prose says 'tests/unit/modules/status/status.test.ts' but the per-phase architecture code places the test at 'src/modules/status/__tests__/status.routes.test.ts'. The canonical dependencies block does not specify the test file path.
options: Use tests/unit/modules/status/status.test.ts as stated in the intent prose
Use src/modules/status/__tests__/status.routes.test.ts as shown in the per-phase architecture code
